expectations.text: The review identifies at least one concrete issue with file and line reference
expectations.evidence: The review contains 10 issues, each with a file reference and most with a line number. Examples: 'lib/matplotlib/tests/test_backend_ps.py' line 292 (global rcParams contamination), 'lib/matplotlib/backends/backend_ps.py' line 952 (missing newpath, single fill, missing decorator), 'lib/matplotlib/rcsetup.py' lines 284 and 0.

expectations.text: Every critical or warning issue includes a specific suggested fix, not just a description of the problem
expectations.evidence: All 7 critical/warning issues contain explicit fixes: (1) 'Must use `with mpl.rc_context({'ps.fonttype': 0}):` (or @pytest.mark.parametrize / @mpl.style.context)'; (2) 'requires a `.. versionadded:: 3.N` note and a what's-new RST under `doc/release/next_whats_new/ps_text_as_path.rst`'; (3) 'must be documented under `doc/api/next_api_changes/behavior_changes/`'; (4) 'Add `self._pswriter.write("newpath\n")` immediately after the `gsave`'; (5) 'emit `fill` (or `eofill`) after each individual glyph sub-path'; (6) 'Add `@_log_if_debug_on` for consistency'; (7) 'should either warn when both options are active or document the interaction clearly'.

expectations.text: The review does not contain false positives (flagged issues that are actually correct code)
expectations.evidence: All flagged issues are substantiated by the diff. (1) Test state pollution confirmed: `mpl.rcParams["ps.fonttype"] = 0` set globally without cleanup in test diff. (2) Missing what's-new RST confirmed: no doc/release/* files in PR diff. (3) Missing API change RST confirmed: no doc/api/* files in PR diff. (4) Missing newpath confirmed: `_draw_text_as_path` uses `gsave` but no `newpath`. (5) Single fill confirmed in diff: `self._pswriter.write("fill\n")` appears once after all glyphs. (6) Missing @_log_if_debug_on confirmed: the new method has no decorator in the diff. The nit about `Path(verts * 64, codes)` then `.scale(scale / 64.0)` is correctly identified as redundant multiplication/division. No false positives found.

expectations.text: The review checks code changes against repo-specific conventions from the skill
expectations.evidence: The review checks multiple matplotlib-specific conventions: (1) `@_log_if_debug_on` decorator that 'every other draw method in RendererPS carries' (logging convention); (2) rc_context scoping for test isolation (test patterns); (3) what's-new RST and API-change RST requirements (release note conventions from skill's 'release-note requirements' responsibility); (4) docstring requirement. These align with the skill's mandate to check 'naming, imports, logging, test patterns, docstring quality, release-note requirements'.

expectations.text: The review verdict (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT) is appropriate to the severity of issues found
expectations.evidence: Verdict is REQUEST_CHANGES. With 3 critical issues — global test state pollution, missing what's-new RST, and missing API-change RST — REQUEST_CHANGES is clearly appropriate. The issues are not cosmetic and must be resolved before merge.

expectations.text: The review was posted to GitHub as a formal review with inline comments
expectations.evidence: Verified via mcp__github__get_pull_request_reviews: the reviews list for matplotlib/matplotlib#31218 is empty ([]). The review exists only as a local review.json file. No formal GitHub review with inline comments was submitted to the PR.

expectations.text: All files changed in PR #31218 were examined before forming conclusions
expectations.evidence: PR #31218 changed 5 files: backend_ps.py, matplotlibrc, rcsetup.py, rcsetup.pyi, test_backend_ps.py. The review summary explicitly states 'Three rcParam files are properly updated (rcsetup.py, matplotlibrc, rcsetup.pyi)' showing matplotlibrc and rcsetup.pyi were examined. Issues reference test_backend_ps.py and backend_ps.py and rcsetup.py. All 5 changed files are accounted for.

claims.claim: The PR introduces a new `_draw_text_as_path` method in RendererPS
claims.evidence: Confirmed in the diff: a new method `def _draw_text_as_path(self, gc, x, y, s, prop, angle, ismath):` is added in backend_ps.py starting around line 952.
Confirmed in rcsetup.py diff: `def validate_ps_fonttype(s):` is a new function added alongside the existing `validate_fonttype`.

claims.claim: Three rcParam files are properly updated (rcsetup.py, matplotlibrc, rcsetup.pyi)
claims.evidence: All three files appear in the PR diff with relevant changes: rcsetup.py adds validate_ps_fonttype and updates validators; matplotlibrc adds '# or Type 0 (path, converts text to outlines)'; rcsetup.pyi adds 'def validate_ps_fonttype(s: Any) -> int: ...'.

claims.claim: The PR is missing required documentation files (what's-new and API-change RST notes)
claims.evidence: The PR diff contains only 5 files (backend_ps.py, matplotlibrc, rcsetup.py, rcsetup.pyi, test_backend_ps.py). No files under doc/release/next_whats_new/ or doc/api/next_api_changes/ appear, confirming the documentation files are absent.

claims.claim: The test pollutes global rcParams state
claims.evidence: Test diff shows `mpl.rcParams["ps.fonttype"] = 0` at line 292 of test_backend_ps.py set globally at function level with no cleanup, no rc_context, and no fixture teardown.

claims.claim: The single trailing `fill` may interact poorly with nonzero winding rule for complex glyphs
claims.evidence: Confirmed in diff: after all glyph sub-paths are written, a single `self._pswriter.write("fill\n")` is called. PostScript's fill uses nonzero winding rule by default, and whether holes in glyphs like 'O' render correctly depends on consistent winding direction from FreeType.

claims.claim: `Path(verts * 64, codes)` followed by `.scale(scale / 64.0)` has cancelling operations
claims.evidence: Confirmed in diff: `path = Path(verts * 64, codes)` then `Affine2D().scale(scale / 64.0).translate(...)` — the multiply by 64 and divide by 64 cancel, so the net effect is `Path(verts, codes)` with `Affine2D().scale(scale)`. This is a real code smell.

suggestions.assertion: The review was posted to GitHub as a formal review with inline comments
suggestions.reason: This is the most critical expectation but also the easiest to definitively verify — a quick API call shows 0 reviews posted. The eval correctly tests this, but given that the skill's entire purpose is to post reviews, a failure here arguably means the skill didn't complete its job. Consider making this expectation weight higher or making it a hard gate that causes the eval to short-circuit.

No assertion checks that the inline comment line numbers correspond to actual changed lines in the diff. The review references line 952 for multiple issues, but the diff only adds code at that approximate location. An assertion like 'all issue line references correspond to lines in the PR diff' would catch hallucinated line references.

No assertion checks whether the review missed significant issues actually present in the diff. For example: the dead code guard in draw_mathtext (flagged as a nit) could have been rated more severely. More importantly, there's no check that the review didn't miss issues visible in the diff.

eval_feedback.overall: The evals are solid and test meaningful outcomes. The main gap is the missing GitHub posting assertion is the single most important behavioral outcome (did the skill do anything useful?) and it fails here. The content-quality assertions (issues have file+line, fixes are specific, no false positives) are well-designed and genuinely discriminating.
